initial_survey:
Out of the following courses, please pick your 5 favorites.

We will be playing Keller, Meadowbrook, Braemar, and Baker as 4 pre-selected courses. We will be voting on which other 4 courses to pick.

To be clear, "Meadowbrook twice" and "Keller twice" would mean we will play either of those courses a second time within the season.

Summer Solstice Scramble event: Would you rather play with your teammate in a 2-man scramble or pair up with another team of 2 for a 4-man scramble? The scramble will use handicap strokes per USGA recommendations and count for league standings.

Any other thoughts, comments, concerns?
Thank you for filling out the survey!
Edinburgh, Inver Wood, Highland National, Meadowbrook twice, Keller twice
Chaska Town Course, Inver Wood, Highland National, Keller twice
4-man scramble with another team as partners
Edinburgh, Inver Wood, Highland National, Meadowbrook twice, Keller twice
Edinburgh, Chaska Town Course, Pioneer Creek, Inver Wood, Keller twice
4-man scramble with another team as partners

Edinburgh, Chaska Town Course, Pioneer Creek, Inver Wood, Highland National
4-man scramble with another team as partners
Edinburgh, Chaska Town Course, Pioneer Creek, Meadowbrook twice, Keller twice
4-man scramble with another team as partners
Beasssssttttty doeeeee. Was torn between baker twice and Chaska town. Chaska prices are wild. I can also plug with Braemar Tee Times
Edinburgh, Chaska Town Course, Pioneer Creek, Highland National, Keller twice
Would like to limit to playing places twice, but Keller is goated so

Form Responses 9: On a scale from 1-10, how interested are you in participating in the 2025 season? 1 being "Can't or Won't participate / 10 being 100% in for next season
What were your favorite 3 courses - considering factors like course conditions, convenience, and cost
What were your LEAST favorite 3 courses - considering factors like course conditions, convenience, and cost

Perhaps you think the league format (2v2 match play) should be changed - If so, what would your suggestion be? Also considering changing the playoff match format.
Not required.
Do you think the league buy-in should increase, decrease, or stay the same? (currently $250)

This season, we had a 4-man scramble event as one of the "matches" for the season. Other options include 2-man medley, 2-man scramble, or something else, entirely. How would you suggest filling the extra week during the season?
Are there any golf courses you would definitely want to add for next season?
What is/are your biggest complaint(s) about the league? Do you have a solid solution for this issue?
What other changes could make the league more enjoyable for you or others?

As the commissioner I am wanting to change the handicap system for next season: Any 9-hole league rounds will be entered as 18-hole scores + 1 stroke (for example, a 9 hole round of 40 would basically be entered as an 81 in the USGA GHIN system). This is intended to better reflect your demonstrated ability in competitive league rounds.

Do you support this change? If not, please state a compelling reason why this is unfair or a bad idea.
Thank you for filling out this survey in order to improve our golf league. We will likely a few teams to fill for next season; if you have any friends interested, let me know!
Gross Natl, Highland Natl, Pioneer Creek
Playoffs: maybe low ball, low total or just straight up best-ball
Merch and a trophy. Better skins payouts.
Gross Natl, Baker Natl, Pioneer Creek
Inver (lots of guys have free pass); como;
Season kick off party/end of year party;
Something that rewards the overall team win. Rather than the bonus point for winning the solo match, a team point(s)
Get rid of the "8th week" of the season; just play 7 matches, instead

Just scheduling remains “difficult”, only thing would be if way prior to the season we determine a “league night” (ex. Tuesdays) just so people try to keep their schedules open( like they would for softball)
If you like this, I like this.
We should do a league happy hour this winter
People who can’t find time to play, so you ax them lol. 😂 I think you know who the problem people are
Breakfast balls always on the table 😎
Yes, I hate the the handicaps new system. 9 hole rounds do nothing
I probably could find some people if need be.
Buy a simulator for your backyard and host the league at your house once a week
Finding a time that works for 4 people. The solution would be to quit the league and get an inverwood membership.
Only courses 15 minutes from my house
SAGL gear. Champions dinner. Get Ben in the league. Force slick to stay in.
Just my partner when I hit a bad shot
Handicap matchups can make it hard to be competitive. I do not that’s golf.
None that I can think of.
I feel like handicaps should be maxed out at like 22. Some of them were too high at the beginning. Nobody playing league golf should be a 25 haha
none, enjoyed the hell out of it
I’d say yes but no +1 stroke. Some people may even play better on the back. Plus im trying to get to get close a scratch so selfishly this will make that harder lol.
Thanks big dog, honored to be in the league.
TBD - I would also support a smaller course set up and do two matches at a course or all courses.
Lawyers! So hard to schedule. I jest - not real issues. Just need to stay on top of scheduling to get them in. We all get busy.
Wouldn’t mind one day a week - it can be hard with travel but if we called ahead on tried to get 4 or 5 straight tee times once a month or something that could help get one match done all at once.
You’re the man. Thanks for all your work ! Always a fun time on the course with new and old friends.
Set days we try and schedule
Not much.. pretty dope as is.. maybe get rid of 4 man scramble so a little more movement from that mid season tourney
Idk. My variance on 9 vs 18 is a lot so it’s to my detriment to do that
I honestly didn’t hear a lot of complaints. Hardest part was finding a day to play an opponent because people are busy
If I still live in MN I would love to play in the league!
Keep the league format the same
